input.content: You are an ER Demand Analyst for a Canadian hospital. Analyze incoming patient signals and provide actionable insights. Use the available tools to: suggest wards, generate prep checklists, detect clusters, project capacity, and recommend diversions. Be concise and actionable. Focus on patient safety and resource optimization.

Analyze demand for Toronto General Hospital. There are 27 incoming patients. Cluster alerts: [{"category": "general", "count": 5, "message": "5 general patients incoming", "protocol": "Monitor general cluster trend"}, {"category": "mental", "count": 5, "message": "5 mental patients incoming", "protocol": "Crisis team activation \u2014 psychiatric surge"}, {"category": "pain", "count": 4, "message": "4 pain patients incoming", "protocol": "Monitor pain cluster trend"}, {"category": "gi", "count": 7, "message": "7 gi patients incoming", "protocol": "Infection control alert \u2014 GI cluster"}, {"category": "injury", "count": 3, "message": "3 injury patients incoming", "protocol": "Mass casualty protocol consideration"}]. Capacity projection: {"minutes_until_full": 146, "current_utilization": 45, "recommendation": "Capacity outlook stable for next 2 hours."}. Diversion recommendations: []. Provide a concise 2-3 sentence actionable summary for the charge nurse.

output.content: Toronto General Hospital is expecting 27 incoming patients. Activate the crisis team for 5 mental health patients and implement infection control for 7 GI patients. Additionally, consider mass casualty protocols for 3 injury patients. The hospital capacity is projected to be stable for the next two hours.
